svg.title: Process enforcement — four layers, soft to hard
svg.desc: Four-layer stack of Plan Marshall process-enforcement mechanisms ordered from soft (skill-loaded text the LLM can still ignore) to hard (mechanical gate that exits non-zero). Layer 1 is the persona-plan-marshall-agent hard rules loaded with every dispatch. Layer 2 is the script-executor proxy plus manage-* funnel through which all .plan/ access flows. Layer 3 is the per-phase Q-Gate findings checkpoint resolved before the next phase entry. Layer 4 is the phase-handshake invariant capture-and-verify protocol that refuses to advance the lifecycle on drift.
svg.text: from skill-loaded guidance the LLM can still ignore … to a mechanical gate that exits non-zero on drift
Skill-loaded text on every dispatch — `.plan/` scripts-only · one Bash command · no shell constructs · architecture-resolve
Strength: guidance · the LLM can violate it (and sometimes does)
Strength: permission-allowlist · unrecognised notation errors out · direct file ops prompt
Layer 3 · Q-Gate findings · resolved at phase entry
Outline + plan + lesson Q-Gates write to the findings store; next phase's entry refuses unresolved findings
Strength: phase checkpoint · LLM must resolve each finding before continuing
Capture at phase completion (git SHA, dirty count, task hash, blocking-findings sum, config hash) · verify at entry
Strength: mechanical gate · drift returns `status: drift`, `--strict` exits 1, transition refuses to advance
Each layer assumes the layers above it can be bypassed. The handshake is the last line — it catches what every higher layer missed.
Small surface (one rules file · one script proxy · one Q-Gate · one handshake registry) — load-bearing impact at every phase boundary.
